_about: MidWest-VR curated K-12 catalog. Pull requests welcome at github.com/DW-K7305/midwest-vr. Each entry is hand-vetted. apk_url + apk_sha256 are required for `source: sideload` entries.

apps.name: Open Brush
apps.publisher: Icosa Foundation (community fork of Google Tilt Brush)
apps.short_desc: Paint in 3D space — open-source Tilt Brush fork. Beloved creative tool.
apps.long_desc: Open Brush lets students paint full 3D environments around themselves. The community fork of Google's Tilt Brush is fully open-source and runs natively on Quest. Outstanding for art class, geometry visualization, set design, and free-time creativity.

apps.name: Mission: ISS
apps.short_desc: Tour the actual International Space Station in microgravity.
apps.long_desc: NASA-backed free experience. Students float through the actual ISS, dock the SpaceX Dragon capsule, and learn about life in space from real astronaut narration. Perfect for space science units.

apps.name: Anne Frank House VR
apps.publisher: Force Field VR / Anne Frank Stichting
apps.short_desc: Walk through the Secret Annex in 1944 Amsterdam.
apps.long_desc: An award-winning historical VR experience produced in partnership with the Anne Frank Foundation. Students experience the hiding place of Anne Frank's family with thoughtful, age-appropriate narration. Pairs powerfully with Holocaust education units.

apps.name: The Body VR: Journey Inside a Cell
apps.short_desc: Travel through the bloodstream and inside a human cell.
apps.long_desc: A free anatomy experience that takes students through veins and inside cells to see organelles in action. Great for middle/high school biology. Available on Quest store.

apps.name: First Hand
apps.short_desc: Hand-tracking demo. Magical first-VR experience for students.
apps.long_desc: Meta's free hand-tracking demo. No controllers needed — students use just their hands. The cleanest 'first 5 minutes in VR' experience available, especially for elementary students.

apps.name: Wander
apps.short_desc: Google-Street-View-style world travel in VR. Geography classes love it.
apps.long_desc: Stand anywhere on Earth and look around in 3D. Wander uses Google Street View imagery so students can visit landmarks, walk a foreign capital, or explore biomes. Free tier covers most use cases.

apps.name: Gorilla Tag
apps.short_desc: Free, social, physical tag. Massive K-12 hit.
apps.long_desc: Climb, run, and tag like a gorilla using only your arms. Free, hugely popular with elementary and middle-school students, and a legitimate workout. Many districts approve it for break time / gym class.

apps.name: Gym Class - Basketball
apps.short_desc: 5v5 pickup basketball in VR. Gets kids moving.
apps.long_desc: A free social VR basketball game. Kids physically jump, dribble, and shoot. Great for PE, gym class, or after-school programs.

apps.name: YouTube VR
apps.short_desc: Watch any 360°/VR video and any normal YouTube video on a virtual screen.
apps.long_desc: Massive content library with thousands of free educational 360° videos: NASA, National Geographic, history reenactments, virtual field trips. Probably the highest content-per-minute educational tool on Quest.

apps.name: Rec Room
apps.short_desc: Free social VR with parental controls. Mini-games, creativity, hangouts.
apps.long_desc: A massive free social VR platform. Students can create rooms, play paintball, do escape rooms, and build content. Robust parental/junior account controls — schools can lock to friends-only or family rooms.

apps.name: Apollo 11 VR
apps.short_desc: Re-live the Apollo 11 mission from launch to lunar landing.
apps.long_desc: Documentary-style VR experience covering the entire Apollo 11 mission. Students experience Saturn V launch, lunar approach, and Neil Armstrong's first steps. Excellent for space science and 20th-century US history.

apps.name: Beat Saber Demo
apps.short_desc: Free demo of the rhythm classic. PE / movement programs.
apps.long_desc: The free demo of the rhythm classic. Slice incoming blocks to the beat with light sabers — extremely physical, hugely engaging, widely used in adaptive PE programs.

apps.name: Titans of Space PLUS
apps.short_desc: Tour our solar system to true scale. Astronomy fundamentals.
apps.long_desc: A guided tour of the planets at near-true scale. Students sit in a spacecraft cockpit and watch as the size and distance of celestial bodies are visualized in a way 2D textbooks simply can't match.

apps.name: ISS National Lab Tour
apps.short_desc: Educational walkthroughs of NASA experiences and lessons.
apps.long_desc: NASA-curated free experiences for STEM education. Pairs nicely with curriculum from NASA's STEM Engagement office.

apps.name: National Geographic Explore VR
apps.short_desc: Photographer's expeditions to Antarctica and Machu Picchu.
apps.long_desc: Take a National Geographic photographer's role on guided expeditions. Antarctic ice climbing and a Machu Picchu archaeological dig. Beautiful for geography, ecology, and exploration units.
